title: Add Go formatting check with gofumpt to CI
summary: Review #1 feedback from sourcery-ai[bot] fully addressed: (1) format-check CI job scoped to git-tracked Go files via `git ls-files '*.go' | xargs -r gofumpt -l` instead of `gofumpt -l .`, (2) GOFUMPT_VERSION wired into local dev via new Makefile targets (`make fmt`, `make format-check`, `make install-gofumpt`) with matching `GOFUMPT_VERSION ?= v0.9.2`. Documentation updated to reflect both changes. No existing CI jobs modified.

tests_checklist.item: CI format-check job logic is correct (exits 1 on violations, prints diff)
Makefile format-check mirrors CI check logic
Makefile fmt applies formatting to tracked files only

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Makefile section structure (section headers, .PHONY, comments)
Feature ID (CC-0053) included in all new comments
GOFUMPT_VERSION consistent between CI (v0.9.2) and Makefile (v0.9.2)
No dead code or unused variables
git ls-files scoping consistent between CI and Makefile targets

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in changes
No command injection vectors (inputs are static glob patterns)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
No existing CI jobs modified (diff shows only format-check changes)
Version pinning follows established CI pattern (env vars in ci.yaml)
Makefile targets follow existing structure conventions

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic (CI and Makefile share approach but serve different contexts)

fail_fast_checklist.item: format-check exits 1 on first violation batch
Makefile format-check provides actionable fix instruction ('make fmt')

defensive_checklist.item: xargs -r prevents execution when no Go files found (GNU coreutils)

suggested_improvements: Makefile targets `fmt` and `format-check` use `xargs -r` which is GNU-specific. BSD xargs (macOS default) does not support `-r` and would fail with 'illegal option'. This is acceptable for CI (ubuntu-latest) and unlikely to cause issues in practice (the repo always has .go files), but macOS contributors would see an error. Consider documenting `brew install findutils` as a prerequisite or replacing `xargs -r` with a portable alternative like `xargs` without `-r` (safe here since the repo always has tracked .go files).
